validated_commands.benchmark: uv run py.test tests/test_processing.py tests/test_filters.py --codspeed -k benchmark --override-ini='addopts='

open_prs.title: [Perf Improver] perf: compute np.square once for full block and quarter block
open_prs.codspeed_result: momentary +7%, short_term +7%, lra +7% per individual benchmarks; overall +2% (below 10% threshold so shows 'does not alter performance' in PR status)

closed_prs.outcome: CLOSED without merge by nocarryr. CodSpeed overall showed 1% improvement (+3% momentary/short_term, +2% lra per individual benchmarks) but below 10% threshold so showed 'not alter performance'. Lesson: Python dispatch overhead reductions don't show up as strongly in CodSpeed instruction counting.
MERGED. Algorithmic O(n^2 log n) -> O(n log n) change showed 37% improvement in CodSpeed.

optimization_backlog.desc: reset() zeros all MAX_BLOCKS (144000) elements even for short measurements; only need to zero [:block_index]
optimization_backlog.impact: 63x faster reset, ~143us saved per reset call (after 50 blocks)
optimization_backlog.notes: Doesn't help benchmarks (teardown not timed). Good user-facing improvement for repeated reset() calls. NOT CodSpeed-detectable.

optimization_backlog.desc: _calc_gating does O(block_index) numpy work per block when rel_threshold changes; O(n^2) total. Could use sorted list + suffix_sum for O(log n) per block.
optimization_backlog.impact: Only affects long sessions (>1000 blocks). For benchmark with ~46 blocks, n^2 is only ~2116 tiny ops.
optimization_backlog.feasibility: low (complex: need suffix-sum structure for weighted sum query)
optimization_backlog.notes: Not CodSpeed-visible for short benchmarks. Real-world benefit only for hour-long streams.

optimization_backlog.desc: lk_log10() uses np.log10 for scalars; math.log10 is 3.2x faster for Python-level floats
optimization_backlog.impact: ~54ms for momentary benchmark, but probably won't show strongly in CodSpeed
optimization_backlog.notes: Changes return type from np.floating to Python float. Functional but type annotation impact. May show small improvement in CodSpeed individual benchmarks.

perf_notes: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' status = overall improvement below 10% threshold. Individual benchmarks may still show meaningful gains (e.g., PR #95: +7% each in momentary/short_term/lra; PR #89: +3% in momentary/short_term).
To see individual benchmark results, follow the CodSpeed link in the PR comment (html anchor).
Only algorithmic changes (O(n^2) -> O(n)) reliably exceed the 10% threshold.
np.mean Python dispatch overhead (~4us per call) is REAL but NOT detected strongly by CodSpeed simulation
Using ndarray.sum(axis=1) instead of np.sum(arr, axis=1) bypasses fromnumeric._wrapreduction
np.square(full_block) reuse (PR #95): +7% in momentary/short_term/lra per CodSpeed individual benchmarks (below 10% overall threshold)
pytest-codspeed teardown (reset()) is NOT included in benchmark timing
cProfile shows _calc_lra was 39% of process_block time before incremental LRA optimization
reset() zeros 144000 elements even for short measurements; optimization: zero only [:block_index]
math.log10 is 3.2x faster than np.log10 for scalar Python floats (0.151 vs 0.485 us)
Benchmark processes ~46 blocks per round; 5ch x 48kHz; gate_size=19200 (0.4s)
checked_off_items: perf-assist/incremental-lra PR (checked off in monthly activity 2026-04)
PR #95 single-sq-pass review (checked off by maintainer in monthly activity 2026-04)

infrastructure_notes.codspeed_threshold: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' = below 10% overall threshold across the whole suite. Individual benchmarks accessible via CodSpeed link at bottom of PR comment. Always check individual results, not just pass/fail status.
infrastructure_notes.codspeed_gap: CodSpeed simulation mode counts CPU instructions, not Python interpreter overhead. Small constant-factor improvements (reducing Python function calls, dispatch overhead) don't reliably exceed 10% threshold. Only algorithmic changes scale enough.
infrastructure_notes.benchmark_coverage: All 4 modes covered: integrated, momentary, short_term, lra. Parameterized by sample_rate and num_channels. Uses pedantic() with warmup_rounds=10 and per-mode rounds (300-800). 5 channels, 48kHz, ~46 blocks per round.
infrastructure_notes.what_shows_in_codspeed: Algorithmic changes (O(n^2) -> O(n)) that scale with problem size across rounds show >10% overall. Smaller optimizations may show 1-7% per individual benchmark but won't trigger 'performance improved' status.
